title: Berlin Späti - Quick Summary
items: A **Späti** is Berlin's late-night corner shop. The full word is **Spätkauf**, meaning roughly "late purchase".
Spätis sell **drinks, snacks, cigarettes, basic groceries and emergency small items**, but every shop is different.
They are also part of **Kiez culture**: people often stand outside with a drink, talk, smoke and use the shop as a casual meeting point.
**Sunday rules are complicated.** Some Spätis can open for certain tourist or special-product ranges, but not every Späti can legally sell everything on Sundays.
**Carry cash** for small late-night purchases, even though more Spätis accept cards than before.
If you buy a bottle or can, remember **Pfand**, the German deposit system. Return it or leave it neatly beside a public bin for collectors.
